name: v0
longDescription:
v0 is an AI assistant from Vercel that generates user interface code from natural-language prompts, screenshots, or Figma references. It outputs React components styled with Tailwind CSS, defaulting to shadcn/ui primitives, and lets users iterate on designs through follow-up messages.

Developers and designers use v0 to scaffold landing pages, dashboards, forms, and full app layouts without starting from a blank file. Generated code can be previewed live, tweaked in chat, and copied directly into a project or shipped to a Vercel deployment.

v0 fits naturally into the Next.js and Vercel ecosystem, making it useful for teams already deploying on that stack who want to skip the boilerplate of common UI patterns.

features.title: Prompt to UI
features.description: Describe a screen in plain English and receive a working React and Tailwind component in seconds.

features.title: Iterative chat
features.description: Refine generated UI through follow-up messages that adjust layout, copy, colors, or behavior.

features.title: shadcn/ui defaults
features.description: Components use the popular shadcn/ui library so output drops cleanly into modern Next.js codebases.

features.title: Image and Figma input
features.description: Upload screenshots or paste Figma frames to generate matching component code.

features.title: Vercel deploy
features.description: Ship a generated project to a live Vercel URL with a single click for previews and demos.

useCases: Scaffolding landing page sections and marketing components quickly
Generating dashboards, forms, and admin layouts for prototypes
Turning a screenshot or design mockup into working React code
Exploring multiple UI variations before committing to a design

faq.question: Is v0 free?
faq.answer: v0 has a free tier with monthly credits. Paid plans offer larger context, more credits, and team features.

faq.question: What framework does v0 use?
faq.answer: v0 generates React components with Tailwind CSS, typically targeting Next.js and using shadcn/ui primitives by default.

faq.question: Do I need a Vercel account?
faq.answer: Yes, sign-in with a Vercel account is required to use v0 and access generated chats.

faq.question: Can I use v0 output commercially?
faq.answer: Generated code can be used in your own projects, including commercial ones, subject to Vercel's terms of service.
